rationale: The citing paper explicitly invokes Ding (2007) when explaining that its respondent lottery was designed to be incentive compatible in the GPS consideration experiments. This is tied to a concrete empirical design decision, not just a broad background list of related work. While Ding (2007) is not the conceptual core of the DOC contribution, it meaningfully supports the validity-oriented setup of the data collection.
relation_description: The citing study applies Ding (2007) as methodological precedent for constructing an incentive-aligned, lottery-based respondent mechanism. It transfers that incentive-compatibility logic to its consideration-set measurement context to promote truthful responses.
